abstract:
Results are presented of emissions and lean
blowout measurements in a generic combustor
primary zone for the effects of opposed, circular
combustion air jets of a range of sizes, positioned
half a dome height downstream from the dome. The
primary zone was operated at simulated engine high
power conditions, and representative dome and liner
pressure drops. It is shown that most of the NOx is
generated in the jets. Although an optimum jet size is
identified, the lowest overall emissions resulted f
